channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Panic spreads in eastern Congo's largest city as rebels battling the army close in around Goma
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Panic spread in Goma on Thursday, with M23 rebels steadily inching closer to the city in eastern Congo as they battle the Congolese army.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Wilson And Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House pair introduces bill to end China's preferred trade status
item.description: Legislation to revoke China’s Permanent Normal Trade Relations was introduced Thursday by a pair of bipartisan House lawmakers, building on a Republican effort last year to repeal Beijing’s preferential trade status. The bill would solidify efforts by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Thai LGBTQ+ couples register marriages as law gives them equal status
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Hundreds of LGBTQ+ couples in Thailand made Thursday a life-changing occasion, registering their marriages legally on the first day a law took effect granting them the same rights as heterosexual couples.
item.dc:creator: CHALIDA EKVITTHAYAVECHNUKUL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal will unfold —and why it is so precarious
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israeli troops have pulled back to the edges of Gaza, the first hostages have been released and many Palestinians have returned to what remains of their homes in the first few days of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, SAMY MAGDY and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scholz's challenger vows tough border rules as attack puts migration in German election spotlight
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's opposition leader vowed Thursday to bar people from entering the country without proper papers and to step up deportations if he is elected chancellor next month, as a knife attack by a rejected asylum-seeker spilled over into an election campaign in which he is the front-run
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean investigators call for indictment of detained President Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators asked prosecutors to indict the country's detained President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived imposition of martial law last month, as it accused him of rebellion, abuse of power and obstruction of parliament on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Saudi foreign minister makes first official trip to Lebanon in a decade after years of strained ties
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s top diplomat has visited Lebanon for the first time in a decade following years of strained relations between the oil-rich kingdom and the tiny Mediterranean country.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Amanda Knox gets a final shot at clearing her name of slander in Italy's top court
item.description: ROME (AP) — Amanda Knox has a final shot at clearing her name of the last vestige of criminal wrongdoing when Italy’s highest court on Thursday hears her appeal of a slander conviction for falsely accusing a Congolese bar owner in the 2007 murder of her British flatmate.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Authorities ramp up efforts to protect sea turtles as deaths on India's shores continue to mount
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Dead sea turtles keep washing ashore on India's east coast and environmentalists and authorities are trying frantically to stop the number of 600 dead from getting even higher.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK police make arrest after 5 people are injured in knife attack in south London
item.description: A man has been arrested on suspicion of grievous bodily harm after five people were stabbed on Thursday morning in south London, emergency services said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump heads back to Davos, this time virtually, for elite World Economic Forum gathering
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Donald Trump is coming back to Davos. This time, virtually. The freshly reinaugurated U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican border states prepare migrant shelters as Trump begins deportation campaign
item.description: CIUDAD JUÁREZ, Mexico (AP) — Mexico raised sprawling tents on the U.S. border Wednesday as it braced for President Donald Trump to fulfill his pledge to carry out mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN SILVA REY, JULIE WATSON and FÉLIX MÁRQUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump talks tough with Putin on ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump is turning up the heat on Russian President Vladimir Putin in his first days in power, seeking to pressure Russia’s leader to come to the negotiating table for a deal to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with taxes, tariffs, sanctions if 'ridiculous' Ukraine war not ended
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday threatened to impose tariffs and sanctions on Russia if there is no deal to stop the fighting in Ukraine "soon." "I’m not looking to hurt Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: NFL legends make emotional trip to Israel in push for hostages' release
item.description: Former NFL stars Nick Lowery and Tony Richardson traveled to Israel where they met with leaders and the family of Hamas hostage Naama Levy in an effort to bring attention to the plight of the captives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police investigate whether foreign actors are paying for antisemitic crimes in Australia
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian detectives are investigating whether foreign actors are paying criminals to commit antisemitic attacks in the country, police said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama, familiar with US intervention, bristles at Trump's comments on canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump’s insistence Monday that he wants the Panama Canal back under U.S. control fed nationalist sentiment and worry in Panama, home to the critical trade route and a country familiar with U.S. military intervention.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: More than 80 people were killed in northeast Colombia over the weekend after the government failed to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Russia sanctions ‘likely’ if Moscow doesn’t negotiate Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday it was likely he would impose additional sanctions on Russia if it did not come to the negotiating table to broker a ceasefire in Ukraine. "Sounds likely," Trump said of potential sanctions on Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Stefanik vows to keep the U.S. engaged at U.N. to counter China
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) assured skeptical Senate Democrats during her confirmation hearing as President Trump's pick for U.N. ambassador that she is committed to engaging in the United Nations, saying it’s a priority venue to challenge China’s push for dominance on the global stage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World leaders react as Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Leaders across the globe reacted to Donald Trump once again entering the White House, with some nations issuing messages of congratulations while others took issue with his inaugural comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli president calls Trump a 'true friend' on Inauguration Day, praises his work to release hostages
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog is praising Donald Trump as a "true friend" of Israel, thanking him for his "commitment to bringing all our hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
